video:video.video:title: Rubio tell journalists in India “there’s been some progress made" on Iran
video:video.video:description: U.S. Secretary of State Marco Rubio said Saturday that some progress had been made on negotiations with Iran and that a diplomatic solution remained Washington’s preferred outcome.

video:video.video:title: Israeli strikes hit two buildings in central Gaza, hospital officials and residents say
video:video.video:description: Israel’s military hit two residential buildings in post-warning strikes in central Gaza, leaving two people wounded and major damages, hospital officials and residents said Saturday.

video:video.video:title: Queen Latifah on a ‘Living Single’ revival
video:video.video:description: Will fans get a "Living Single" revival? Here's what Queen Latifah had to say.

video:video.video:title: Penélope Cruz on discussing LGBTQ+ issues in 'The Black Ball'
video:video.video:description: Penélope Cruz says "The Black Ball" follows in the tradition of her seven films with Pedro Almodóvar.

video:video.video:title: Pedro Pascal on collabing with stunt performers as the Mandalorian
video:video.video:description: Pedro Pascal on collaborating with stunt performers and body doubles Lateef Crowder and Brendan Wayne to create Din Djarin, his Disney+ series character now on big screens in "The Mandalorian and Grogu."

video:video.video:title: Ella Bright and Belmont Cameli talk hockey drama 'Off Campus'
video:video.video:description: Don't expect "smooth sailing" for Ella Bright and Belmont Cameli's characters in the hockey romance "Off Campus."

video:video.video:title: Miley Cyrus star on Walk of Fame revealed
video:video.video:description: Donatella Versace and Anya Taylor-Joy joined Miley Cyrus at her Hollywood Walk of Fame ceremony.

video:video.video:title: Miley Cyrus arrives at her Hollywood Walk of Fame ceremony
video:video.video:description: Miley Cyrus receives a star on Hollywood's Walk of Fame after celebrating 20 years of her hit Disney show "Hannah Montana."

video:video.video:title: Gunmen open fire in 2 separate attacks in Honduras, killing at least 25 people
video:video.video:description: At least 25 people were killed Thursday in two separate attacks in Honduras, that authorities blamed on organized crime, including five police officers who were abducted and later found dead near the Guatemalan border.

video:video.video:title: Penélope Cruz walks Cannes final carpet
video:video.video:description: Penélope Cruz walks the final carpet at the Cannes Film Festival, where she premiered "The Black Ball."

video:video.video:title: Fire chief says damaged Southern California chemical tank gets warmer, raising concerns for rupture
video:video.video:description: A damaged chemical storage tank that authorities fear is at risk of exploding or rupturing in Southern California is gradually getting warmer despite efforts to reduce its internal temperature, a fire chief said Saturday.

video:video.video:title: Rami Malek on his musical abilities
video:video.video:description: Rami Malek did not consider himself a musical person — until he starred in "Bohemian Rhapsody." His new film is "The Man I Love."

video:video.video:title: Clashes between protesters and riot police after massive anti-government rally in Belgrade
video:video.video:description: Clashes erupted between protesters and riot police after a massive anti-government rally in Belgrade on Saturday.

video:video.video:title: Israel hits residential building in post-warning strike in central Gaza
video:video.video:description: The Israeli military hit a residential building in a post-warning strike on central Gaza on Saturday, causing serious damage.

video:video.video:title: AP top stories May 23
video:video.video:description: Here's the latest for Saturday, May 23rd: Southern California chemical tank is at risk of failing; Family says Kyle Busch died after severe pneumonia progressed into sepsis; Protestor holds rally in Serbia's Belgrade; Flotilla activists describe beatings by Israeli forces.

video:video.video:title: Tens of thousands join big rally in Belgrade called by Serbia's protesting university students
video:video.video:description: Tens of thousands of people joined a big rally on Saturday called by Serbia's protesting university students aiming to renew pressure on the authoritarian President Aleksandar Vucic after mass demonstrations shook his tight rule last year.

video:video.video:title: Marines conducts a rapid response exercise at the US Embassy in Venezuela's capital
video:video.video:description: The U.S. military conducted a rapid response exercise involving Marines and military aircraft in Venezuela’s capital on Saturday, more than four months after the ouster of then-President Nicolás Maduro.

video:video.video:title: Suspect dead after approaching White House checkpoint with weapon, US Secret Service says
video:video.video:description: A suspect is dead after approaching a White House checkpoint with a weapon, the US Secret Service says.

video:video.video:title: Venezuela's Machado vows another run for presidency and eyes a return from exile by end of 2026
video:video.video:description: Venezuela's Nobel Peace Prize laureate María Corina Machado announced on Saturday that she plans to run for president again and intends to return to her home country before the end of 2026.

video:video.video:title: 'La Bola Negra' directors call Glenn Close during interview
video:video.video:description: “La Bola Negra” directors called Glenn Close during an interview with The Associated Press to inform her on the movie’s success.

video:video.video:title: Pope Leo XIV greets faithful during visit to area near Naples affected by illegal toxic dumping
video:video.video:description: Pope Leo XIV on Saturday greeted one by one families who lost loved ones to illegal toxic dumping in an area near Naples, as many paused to share photographs and other mementos of children and young people who have died or are battling cancer — illnesses tied to a multibillion-dollar criminal racket

video:video.video:title: Meet the hospital dogs that provide full-time care for young sick patients
video:video.video:description: Three facility dogs work full-time at Mount Sinai Kravis Children’s Hospital. Summer, Icee and Muffin support patients with snuggles in bed, being present for difficult procedures and more.

video:video.video:title: Bolivian unions marched to La Paz and clashed with police to protest the nation’s economic crisis
video:video.video:description: Violent clashes unfolded between Bolivian police and members of the Bolivian Worker's Union, in La Paz on Friday, after thousands of protesters marched through the city as the nation's economic crisis deepened.

video:video.video:title: Flotilla activists describe beatings, tasers and mistreatment by Israeli forces
video:video.video:description: Activists detained after their flotilla tried to breach Israel's naval blockade of Gaza have reported mistreatment by Israeli soldiers.

video:video.video:title: 40,000 ordered to evacuate in Southern California after chemical leak at plastics facility
video:video.video:description: About 40,000 people were under evacuation orders and schools shut down Friday in Southern California after a storage tank continued to leak a hazardous chemical used to make plastic parts that officials said could rupture or explode.

video:video.video:title: Over 1.6 million Muslim pilgrims arrive in Saudi Arabia ahead of this year’s Hajj pilgrimage
video:video.video:description: Worshippers from around the world have gathered in Islam’s holiest city amid regional conflicts and tightened restrictions. (AP Video: Baraa Anwer and Altaf Qadri)

video:video.video:title: Burials of Ebola victims continue in eastern Congo under armed escort after community resistance
video:video.video:description: As the second of three Ebola treatment centers was burned in eastern Congo on Saturday, Congolese army soldiers and police in riot gear escorted the ambulances carrying Ebola victims to a Rwampara cemetery.

video:video.video:title: Suspect dead after approaching White House checkpoint with weapon, US Secret Service says
video:video.video:description: A person who approached a White House security checkpoint and began firing at officers has died, according to federal officials. The U.S.

video:video.video:title: Snow cover on Greek mountains halved in four decades according to new report
video:video.video:description: Snowfall is declining in Greece's mountains, leaving the communities that depend on it facing an uncertain future.

video:video.video:title: Britain prepares to deploy to clear mines in the Strait of Hormuz but is waiting for a peace deal
video:video.video:description: President Trump has lambasted many of his allies for failing in his eyes to adequately support his war in Iran but America’s closest, the U.K., has drawn his particular ire.

video:video.video:title: Rubio expects Trump to make further announcement after 'progress' on Iran deal
video:video.video:description: U.S. Secretary of State Marco Rubio said Sunday that “significant progress, although not final progress, has been made” in negotiations over Iran as he met Indian External Affairs Minister Subrahmanyam Jaishankar during a four-day visit to India.

video:video.video:title: Oldest living Pearl Harbor attack survivor, at 106, says getting married was the real victory
video:video.video:description: Freeman Johnson is 106 and the oldest living Pearl Harbor attack survivor. He discusses the raid 84 years ago and subsequent wartime experience, but declares his greatest legacy is raising a family.

video:video.video:title: Deadliest coal mine explosion in China in years kills at least 82 people
video:video.video:description: A gas explosion at a coal mine in China’s northern Shanxi province killed at least 82 people, local officials said Saturday, in what was the country’s deadliest mining accident in recent years.

video:video.video:title: In violent Guayaquil, Ecuador’s young players train behind guards as World Cup hope rises
video:video.video:description: As a child, Piero Hicapié—one of the stars of the Ecuadorian national team—spent a brief stint in one of Barcelona’s youth academies.

video:video.video:title: A suicide bombing near a railway track in southwest Pakistan kills at least 23 people
video:video.video:description: A suicide bomber detonated an explosives-laden vehicle near a railway track as a passenger train passed through the southwestern Pakistani city of Quetta on Sunday, killing at least 23 people and wounding over 70 others, officials said.

video:video.video:title: Live music stirring back to life in Tehran as ceasefire offers fragile respite
video:video.video:description: Bands and orchestras have begun returning to the stage in Tehran, where musicians have struggled to find work since war with the U.S. and Israel broke out in late February.

video:video.video:title: Thousands in Spain’s capital protest increasing housing costs
video:video.video:description: Thousands of people rallied in central Madrid on Sunday against spiraling housing costs that have priced many Spaniards out of the housing market despite a recent economic boom, particularly in cities such as the capital and Barcelona.

video:video.video:title: Ebola 'scouts' raise awareness in Bunia amid eastern Congo outbreak
video:video.video:description: At the central market in Bunia, a city at the heart of the ongoing Ebola outbreak, François Kasereka spoke to a small crowd that has gathered, telling them they should not touch a family member who is sick.

video:video.video:title: AP top stories May 24
video:video.video:description: Here's the latest for Sunday, May 24th: U.S. is close to a deal with Iran to end the war; Pakistan train bombing kills at least two dozen people; China investigates deadly coal mine blast; Kenton Cool may climb Everest again after record 20th ascent.

video:video.video:title: OC Fire official says chemical tank may have cracked potentially lowering explosion risk
video:video.video:description: A damaged chemical tank in Southern California may have cracked — potentially lowering the risk of a cataclysmic explosion — though an evacuation order remains in effect for residents with no timeline on when they can return.

video:video.video:title: President Noboa addresses the nation from Ecuador's National Assembly
video:video.video:description: Ecuador's President Daniel Noboa highlighted on Sunday the extradition of key organized crime leaders and the improvement in economic indicators in Ecuador as he presented the report on his first year in office of the new four-year term.

video:video.video:title: Turkish police storm offices of main opposition CHP party, firing tear gas and rubber bullets
video:video.video:description: Police stormed the offices of Turkey’s main opposition CHP party on Sunday, firing tear gas and rubber bullets at party supporters and officials who had been holed up inside for three days.

video:video.video:title: Red Cross, shelters help Southern California chemical tank evacuees as evacuation order remains
video:video.video:description: A damaged chemical tank in Southern California may have cracked, potentially lowering the risk of a catastrophic explosion, though evacuation orders remain in effect for about 50,000 residents.

video:video.video:title: Britain prepares to deploy to clear mines in the Strait of Hormuz but is waiting for a peace deal
video:video.video:description: The U.K.'s Royal Navy is preparing for a potential mine-clearing operation in the Strait but it's not clear if it will ever be deployed as the U.K says a peace deal needs to be reached first.

video:video.video:title: Send off ceremony held for astronauts in Jiuquan ahead of the launch of Shenzhou 23
video:video.video:description: China launched the Shenzhou 23 spacecraft Sunday night with three astronauts heading to its space station, including one set to stay in space for a year.

video:video.video:title: Katie Aselton and Daveed Diggs explore love and loss in 'Magic Hour'
video:video.video:description: Writer, director and star Katie Aselton and co-star Daveed Diggs discuss 'Magic Hour' - their drama about love, loss and letting go. (May 24)

video:video.video:title: Senior Iran official warns that war could expand and asks opponents to 'turn back from this path'
video:video.video:description: Gen. Mohsen Rezaei, a senior advisor to Iran's supreme leader, warned on Sunday that the Iran war could expand the include the areas of the Red Sea, Bab el-Mandeb, and the Indian Ocean.

video:video.video:title: Details emerge of potential Iran deal which would reopen Strait of Hormuz
video:video.video:description: The United States is close to reaching a deal with Iran that would end the war, reopen the Strait of Hormuz and see Iran give up its stockpile of highly enriched uranium, with the details and timelines to be worked out later, regional officials told The Associated Press on Sunday.

video:video.video:title: Timelapse of Muslim pilgrims circling Kaaba in Mecca ahead of Hajj
video:video.video:description: Around 1.6 million Muslims have gathered in Mecca ahead of the start of Hajj, one of the world’s largest annual religious gatherings, as pilgrims prepared for the beginning of the multi-day pilgrimage amid soaring temperatures and regional tensions.

video:video.video:title: Jim Henson's puppet creature shop now open for tours
video:video.video:description: A puppet-making workshop founded by the legendary creator of the Muppets is drawing back its curtain. Jim Henson’s Creature Shop has opened its doors to public tours.

video:video.video:title: Muslims from around the world gather in Mecca ahead of annual Hajj pilgrimage
video:video.video:description: Around 1.6 million Muslims have gathered in Mecca on Sunday ahead of the start of Hajj, one of the world’s largest annual religious gatherings.

video:video.video:title: Revelers pack a Hong Kong island for a bun festival, a century-old tradition
video:video.video:description: At midnight in Hong Kong’s tiny outlying island of Cheung Chau, 12 contestants raced up a 14-metre bamboo tower covered with 9,000 plastic buns.

video:video.video:title: Fires burn in Kyiv after Russia uses hypersonic missile in mass attack on Ukraine
video:video.video:description: Russia attacked Kyiv with missiles and drones overnight Saturday into Sunday in an intense assault that shook buildings across the city center, including near government offices, residential buildings and schools.

video:video.video:title: Building under construction in Philippines collapses, leaving 1 dead and 21 trapped
video:video.video:description: A nine-story building under construction in a city north of the Philippine capital collapsed before dawn Sunday, leaving at least one Malaysian tourist dead and at least 21 mostly workers trapped in the rubble, officials said.

video:video.video:title: How Rwanda is using DNA traces to protect endangered mountain gorillas
video:video.video:description: Scientists in Rwanda are collecting traces of DNA left behind in soil and water to identify endangered species like mountain gorillas without disturbing their habitat.

video:video.video:title: Drone attacks raise fears for voters as Colombians vote to elect a new president
video:video.video:description: Gladys Marín is unsure if she will vote in Colombia's presidential election due to safety fears. Her town in southwestern Colombia has endured frequent drone attacks, blamed on a rebel faction.

video:video.video:title: Fear and mistrust hamper Ebola response in eastern Democratic Republic of Congo
video:video.video:description: Health authorities have been facing increasing resistance from the community in eastern Congo’s rapidly spreading Ebola outbreak, with two of three Ebola treatment centers having been burned by local residents and burials now being carried out under armed escort.

video:video.video:title: In Sudan’s war economy, gold keeps flowing as miners risk mercury and collapse
video:video.video:description: The men carried metal detectors as they scanned a mountainous area in northern Sudan in search of gold.

video:video.video:title: Marco Rubio visits the Taj Mahal in India, says Iran negotiations ‘a work in progress’
video:video.video:description: U.S. Secretary of State Marco Rubio, on Monday, called U.S.. negotiations with Iran "a work in progress."

video:video.video:title: Slow food: the city that once fed Europe on snails is back in business
video:video.video:description: A farm in Vienna is growing an unusual product - snails. It's reviving a tradition from when the Austrian city was once the snail capital of the world, supplying the best restaurants with molluscs.
